title: Veterans Benefits After a Death: Where to Start
summary: Veterans and their survivors may be eligible for several benefits that families often don't realize exist.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Veterans Benefits After a Death: Where to Start | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Veterans and their survivors may be eligible for several benefits that families often don't realize exist.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Burial benefits, survivor pensions, and dependency compensation are among the possibilities.
Eligibility depends on service history and circumstances of death.
The VA and Veterans Service Organizations can help navigate the specific options.

paragraphs: Depending on service history, benefits may include burial in a national cemetery, a burial allowance, survivor pension (for low-income surviving spouses), and Dependency and Indemnity Compensation in certain circumstances — the specifics depend heavily on individual service history.
Veterans Service Organizations (like the VFW or American Legion) often have accredited representatives who help families navigate VA paperwork at no cost — a genuinely useful, underused resource.

nextSteps: Gather the veteran's discharge paperwork (DD-214) if available.
Contact the VA or a Veterans Service Organization for guidance.
Ask specifically what the family may be eligible for, since it's not always obvious.
disclaimer: General information — the VA or an accredited Veterans Service Organization can confirm specific eligibility.
